retry_reason: refcount_dec always executes and failure (e.g., refcount already zero) indicates a use-after-free bug, not a transient condition; retrying would cause double-put.
contract_summary: Releases a reference on an in_device by decrementing its refcount. This is a put wrapper – the caller must hold a valid reference before calling, and must not access the object after this call unless another reference is held. Pairs with in_dev_hold. Underlying refcount_dec is unconditional and void; the caller cannot detect failure via return value. Calling when the refcount is already zero leads to use-after-free (refcount saturation or object already freed).

judge.reason: The macro's sole purpose is to decrement the refcount of an in_device; it only calls refcount_dec with no additional logic or checks.

check.reason: refcount_dec is called on &(idev)->refcnt, which accesses the refcount_t field refcnt of in_device, then the atomic_t refs inside refcount_t.
